you you yeah I guess it already has been two years yeah because I think I think you moved we switched spots we moved back up here and you move down there right yeah yeah okay yeah it's awesome you know the first three months and I don't know if you remember or more brutal where it's like what am I doing I have no idea and now it's like it's awesome you know now that we're past that he's gonna be 20 20 months and you know we just got lucky he's a pretty calm guy super funny so yeah it's cool yeah yeah it's funny it's one of those things you can't really describe you know because he asked all these questions before the baby's born and so what am I gonna do and then you have this little person it's like yes definitely different and it's awesome yeah when I don't know if you have this but I just made me question what the fuck was I doing with all my time see you know remember before kids you're like oh my god I'm so busy and I'm so tired it's like what was it doing and I forgot you have you have kids right you know you know it's still it's it's still awesome it's fun you know we can hang out and they are independent of me and that's cool that's cool right on well so thanks for filling out the format I'm gonna tell you like a little like snapshot of what we basically broke our old gym so like I'll give you the quick cliff notes so a year ago I signed up with this guy dr. So the 5.42 was basically templated and we would change the portion size based on your weight. And so with this now Dr. Kashi only works with like fitness professionals and Olympians so he created a whole curriculum for us on how to distribute it so that you like to know a lot of like the why I don't want to bore you with. No I do I like it. So the way this is awesome so the way that it works is we need to take you from basically crawling to running so step one is we just have you eating from a food list of whole foods and it's as much as you want if you want to start measuring you can. But then we would take you back to hand portions so Palmer protein fist of edgies cup of carbs and all we're trying to do is two things work on consistency first. Which it sounds like you're already doing then the second step is accuracy. So once you're using your hand we're going to give you a calorie goal and then that's where you start weighing all your food and this becomes cool once you have a calorie goal. You can start mixing in the foods that you really enjoy and it makes it sustainable with 5.42 you had a set menu or you had to eat those foods each day so it wasn't sustainable you know what I mean. And so what worked really well for Anna once you have your calorie goal would give you a protein goal. And for 90% of people that's all you're going to need but what changed for her is like I used to wake up in the morning in the jar of peanut butter would be out from the night before like her coach is this replaced the emotion with math. So now when I bring home pizza she'll look at her phone and be like okay I got 600 calories left off like two pieces. And so so everything super calculated but now she has targets and some nights she'll be like I need 40 grams of fat so she'll make like 40 you know mozzarella sticks in the airfire. And so then that's the big difference so now it's going to be sustainable but once we're we're basically going to build your body up right now it kind of sounds like you're a Prius so you're taking a small amount in and you're burning a small amount we're going to slowly build you up to a Ferrari. Which you're I have no idea where we're going to start but it might be anywhere from like I don't know 13 to 14 under calories based on how much is your eating but we're slowly going to build you up. So then when you don't do go in that recovery phase you're going to have lost the 43 pounds but now you're going to have so many more calories to work with. Does that make sense. So it's basically math right like okay this is how much protein this is how much this is how much this is participating per day. Yeah, and it's so yeah and that's the cool part because it's going to be totally tailored to you. So I have a ton of women that are different shapes forms and sizes. Where before I give you any numbers I'm going to want to have you track these things so I know what you're doing normally. And then I can see like this is roughly if you're only eating 1100 calories a day. I can't put you at what they were telling you like your BMR of 1800 because your body just wouldn't adapt to that and then it just becomes of getting really accurate with measuring. So all those small things that maybe you don't notice throughout the day of like having a snack here having a snack there everything's accounted for. And in some ways it might seem restrictive but it's the total opposite once you plan for this there's no such thing as a bad food as long as you plan for it.
